member.name: T:CtxSignlib.Certificates.CertHelpers
member.summary: Certificate helper utilities for generating, exporting, loading, and (optionally) installing certificates.
member.remarks: These helpers are designed to support deterministic signing/verification workflows and developer tooling. Any interaction with OS certificate stores is explicitly opt-in and clearly marked as platform-specific.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Certificates.CertHelpers.CreateSelfSignedRsa(System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates an in-memory self-signed RSA X.509 certificate suitable for code-signing scenarios.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Certificates.CertHelpers.ExportPfx(System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates.X509Certificate2,System.String)
member.summary: Exports a certificate (including its private key, if present) as a PFX/PKCS#12 byte array.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Certificates.CertHelpers.WritePfxFile(System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates.X509Certificate2,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Writes a certificate as a PFX/PKCS#12 file using an atomic write strategy.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Certificates.CertHelpers.LoadPfxFile(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Loads a PFX/PKCS#12 file using cross-platform-friendly key storage flags intended for signing workflows.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Certificates.CertHelpers.InstallToCurrentUserStore(System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates.X509Certificate2)
member.summary: Installs a certificate into the current user's personal ("My") certificate store.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Certificates.CertHelpers.RemoveFromCurrentUserStore(System.String)
member.summary: Removes certificates from the current user's personal ("My") certificate store that match a given thumbprint.

member.name: T:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.CtxException
member.summary: Represents a CtxSignlib-specific exception that carries stable diagnostic metadata for tooling, logging, and host-level error translation.

member.name: P:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.CtxException.Target
member.summary: Gets the diagnostic target associated with the exception.

member.name: P:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.CtxException.Detail
member.summary: Gets the specific diagnostic detail associated with the exception.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.CtxException.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with a default diagnostic classification.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.CtxException.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified message and a default diagnostic classification.
member.param: The exception message.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.CtxException.#ctor(CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorTarget,CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified diagnostic classification.
member.param: The diagnostic target associated with the exception.
The specific diagnostic detail associated with the exception.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.CtxException.#ctor(CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorTarget,CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail,System.Exception)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified diagnostic classification and inner exception.
member.param: The diagnostic target associated with the exception.
The specific diagnostic detail associated with the exception.
The exception that caused the current exception.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.CtxException.#ctor(System.String,CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorTarget,CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified message and diagnostic classification.
member.param: The exception message.
The diagnostic target associated with the exception.
The specific diagnostic detail associated with the exception.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.CtxException.#ctor(System.String,CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorTarget,CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail,System.Exception)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified message, diagnostic classification, and inner exception.
member.param: The exception message.
The diagnostic target associated with the exception.
The specific diagnostic detail associated with the exception.
The exception that caused the current exception.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.CtxException.ToString
member.summary: Returns a string representation of the current exception, including diagnostic metadata.
member.returns: A string representation of the current exception.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.CtxException.BuildDefaultMessage(CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorTarget,CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail)
member.summary: Creates a fallback message when no explicit message is supplied.
member.param: The diagnostic target associated with the exception.
The specific diagnostic detail associated with the exception.
member.returns: A simple default exception message.

member.name: T:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail
member.summary: Identifies the specific diagnostic detail associated with a library outcome or failure.

remarks.para: provides a stable, machine-readable reason that callers can use alongside and higher-level result values.
Host applications may translate these values into return codes, help topics, remediation guidance, logs, or localized messages.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.None
member.summary: No diagnostic detail is associated with the current outcome.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.Ok
member.summary: The operation completed successfully.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.Generic
member.summary: A general or unspecified diagnostic condition occurred.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.InvalidArguments
member.summary: The supplied arguments, options, or parameters were invalid, incomplete, or inconsistent.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.MissingInput
member.summary: A required value, file, path, or input was not provided.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.FileNotFound
member.summary: A required file or path was not found.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.DirectoryNotFound
member.summary: A required directory was not found.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.FileUnreadable
member.summary: A file or path could not be read.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.AccessDenied
member.summary: Access to a required file, path, or resource was denied.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.InvalidFormat
member.summary: Input data was present but not in a valid or expected format.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.UnsupportedFormat
member.summary: The requested format, algorithm, or input type is not supported.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.PlatformNotSupported
member.summary: The current platform does not support the requested operation.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.InvalidRegex
member.summary: A regular expression or pattern value was invalid.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.InvalidOperation
member.summary: The operation failed because the current state, configuration, or object usage was invalid.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.OperationFailed
member.summary: The operation failed for a reason not covered by a more specific diagnostic detail.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.ResourceMissing
member.summary: A required embedded or internal resource was not found.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.MissingMetadata
member.summary: Required metadata was missing from an otherwise valid operation or result.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.MissingRequiredData
member.summary: Required structured data was missing from the input or payload.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.ConflictingConfiguration
member.summary: A conflict was detected in the supplied configuration, input, or excludes.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.SignatureMissing
member.summary: A required detached signature was not found.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.BadSignature
member.summary: Signature verification failed because the signature content was invalid.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.NoSigner
member.summary: No signer certificate was present in the signature material.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.WrongSigner
member.summary: The signer did not match the expected identity material, such as a thumbprint, pin, or public-key pin.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.PinMissing
member.summary: Required pin material was not provided.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.InvalidPin
member.summary: Provided pin material was invalid or could not be parsed.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.ManifestMissing
member.summary: A manifest file was not found.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.InvalidManifest
member.summary: Manifest content was invalid, malformed, or could not be parsed.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.FileMissing
member.summary: A file required by the manifest was missing.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.FileOutsideRoot
member.summary: A file referenced by the manifest resolved outside the allowed root.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.FileNotInManifest
member.summary: A file was encountered that is not present in the manifest.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.HashMismatch
member.summary: File content did not match the expected hash value.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.TrustBoundaryViolation
member.summary: A path or resource violated the library trust boundary rules.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.CertificateLoadFailed
member.summary: Certificate material could not be loaded.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.InvalidCertificate
member.summary: Certificate material was invalid, malformed, or unusable.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.PrivateKeyMissing
member.summary: Required private key material was missing or inaccessible.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.AuthenticationFailed
member.summary: Authentication or signer identity validation failed.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.CryptographicFailure
member.summary: A cryptographic operation failed.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.Timeout
member.summary: A timeout occurred while waiting for a required operation or resource.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorDetail.InvalidTime
member.summary: A time-based validation condition failed, such as a missing, invalid, expired, or out-of-range time value.

member.name: T:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorTarget
member.summary: Identifies the diagnostic target associated with a library operation, input category, or failure domain.

remarks.para: provides a stable, machine-readable classification that callers can use to determine the functional area associated with an outcome, error, or diagnostic condition.
Host applications may translate these values into user-facing help, remediation guidance, logging categories, exit codes, or other presentation-specific behavior.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorTarget.None
member.summary: No diagnostic target is associated with the current outcome.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorTarget.General
member.summary: Indicates a general or uncategorized diagnostic condition.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorTarget.Operation
member.summary: Indicates a diagnostic condition related to the requested operation or feature entry point.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorTarget.Arguments
member.summary: Indicates a diagnostic condition related to arguments, options, or parameter input.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorTarget.FileSystem
member.summary: Indicates a diagnostic condition related to files, paths, directories, or resource access.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorTarget.Pin
member.summary: Indicates a diagnostic condition related to public key pins or pin material.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorTarget.Certificate
member.summary: Indicates a diagnostic condition related to certificates or certificate material.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorTarget.Manifest
member.summary: Indicates a diagnostic condition related to manifest creation, parsing, validation, or trust-boundary enforcement.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorTarget.Signing
member.summary: Indicates a diagnostic condition related to signing operations.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorTarget.Verification
member.summary: Indicates a diagnostic condition related to verification operations.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorTarget.Authentication
member.summary: Indicates a diagnostic condition related to authentication, signer identity, or trust validation.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorTarget.Cryptography
member.summary: Indicates a diagnostic condition related to cryptographic operations or cryptographic material.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ErrorTarget.Time
member.summary: Indicates a diagnostic condition related to time-based validation or timing constraints.

member.name: T:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ManifestPartialVerificationResult
member.summary: Represents the categorized result of a manifest verification operation.
member.remarks: This object records the observed state of files listed in a manifest. The verification engine populates the file lists and wrappers determine the policy outcome (strict vs partial).

member.name: P:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ManifestPartialVerificationResult.PassedFiles
member.summary: Gets the list of files that were present and matched the expected hash.

member.name: P:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ManifestPartialVerificationResult.MissingFiles
member.summary: Gets the list of files that were listed in the manifest but were not present.

member.name: P:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ManifestPartialVerificationResult.FailedFiles
member.summary: Gets the list of files that were present but whose hash did not match the manifest.

member.name: P:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ManifestPartialVerificationResult.UnreadableFiles
member.summary: Gets the list of files that existed but could not be read.

member.name: P:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ManifestPartialVerificationResult.InvalidSyntaxFiles
member.summary: Gets the list of files whose verification could not be completed because the syntax of a rule or instruction associated with that file was invalid.
member.remarks: Examples include malformed regex patterns or other malformed per-file verification syntax that prevents a safe hash evaluation. These files are not considered matched or mismatched; they are unverifiable.

member.name: P:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ManifestPartialVerificationResult.Success
member.summary: Gets or sets the overall success state determined by the calling verification mode.

member.name: P:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ManifestPartialVerificationResult.ManifestAuthenticated
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the manifest signature authentication step succeeded.
member.remarks: For unsigned manifest verification this will remain false. Signed-manifest verification paths set this to true after successful CMS signature validation.

member.name: P:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ManifestPartialVerificationResult.IsStrictlyValid
member.summary: Indicates whether the result satisfies strict verification semantics.
member.remarks: Strict mode requires: - no missing files - no failed files - no unreadable files - no invalid syntax files

member.name: P:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ManifestPartialVerificationResult.IsPartiallyValid
member.summary: Indicates whether the result satisfies partial verification semantics.
member.remarks: Partial mode allows missing files but still requires: - no failed files - no unreadable files - no invalid syntax files

member.name: P:CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ManifestPartialVerificationResult.ExpectedHashByPath
member.summary: Maps manifest-relative file paths to their expected SHA-256 value.
member.remarks: This is used internally so legacy APIs can rebuild the original failure dictionary structure grouped by expected hash.

member.name: T:CtxSignlib.Functions
member.summary: Global shared utility functions used throughout the library.
member.remarks:
This class is intended to be used as a global/static helper surface so calls can be made unqualified (for example, Null(value) instead of Functions.Null(value)).
Methods in this class are designed to be deterministic and low-level. Any side-effects (for example file I/O) are called out explicitly in the member documentation.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.TryParseArgs(System.String[],System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.String,System.String}@,System.StringComparer)
member.summary: Parses command-line arguments into a dictionary of key/value pairs.
member.param: The raw command-line arguments (typically string[] args from Main).
On success, receives a dictionary containing parsed values. Keys are case-insensitive by default.
Optional dictionary key comparer. If not supplied, is used.
member.returns: Always returns true. (This method is structured as a "Try" pattern to keep call sites uniform.)
member.remarks:
Supports:
--key=value
--key value
--flag (stored as "true")
-k value
-abc (expanded to -a -b -c, each stored as "true")
Positional arguments stored as _0, _1, ...

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.HasArg(System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.String,System.String},System.String)
member.summary: Returns true if a parsed args dictionary contains a given argument name.
member.param: Parsed args dictionary produced by .
Argument name (without leading dashes).

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.GetArg(System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.String,System.String},System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Gets an argument value from a parsed args dictionary, returning a default if missing.
member.param: Parsed args dictionary.
Argument name (without leading dashes).
Value to return if the key is not present.
member.returns: The stored value, or if missing.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.GetArgBool(System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.String,System.String},System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Gets a boolean argument value from a parsed args dictionary.
member.param: Parsed args dictionary.
Argument name (without leading dashes).
Value to return if missing.
member.returns: true if present as a flag (--name) or if the value is one of: 1, true, yes, on (case-insensitive). Otherwise false.
member.remarks: If the argument exists but has an empty value, it is treated as true.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.IsSubPathOf(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Determines whether is located under (or is equal to it), using full-path normalization.
member.param: The root directory to test against.
The candidate path that may be a descendant of .
member.returns: true if is the same as or is contained beneath it; otherwise, false.
member.remarks: Comparison is case-insensitive on Windows and case-sensitive on non-Windows platforms. Trailing separators are handled to prevent false positives (for example, /a/b2 is not treated as under /a/b).

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.NormalizePath(System.String)
member.summary: Normalizes a path string for basic consistency by trimming whitespace and converting all directory separators to the current platform's separator.
member.param: The path to normalize.
member.returns: A trimmed path using . Returns if is null/whitespace.
member.remarks: This does not validate existence, resolve relative segments, or enforce absolute paths.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.NormalizeManifestPath(System.String)
member.summary: Normalizes a manifest-relative path into a canonical form: forward slashes, no leading ./ segments, and no leading slash.
member.param: A relative path as supplied by a manifest or caller.
member.returns: A canonical relative path suitable for deterministic manifest storage and comparison. Returns if input is null/whitespace.
member.remarks: This function does not attempt to resolve .. segments; callers should ensure their own policy on traversal.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.ParsePublicKeyBytes(System.String)
member.summary: Parses raw public key bytes from a PEM, base64, or hex representation.

remarks.para: The --pin contract is SubjectPublicKeyInfo (SPKI) bytes (the same bytes represented by PEM -----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----). This is the canonical input for public-key pinning.
Accepts:

item.description: PEM SubjectPublicKeyInfo ("BEGIN PUBLIC KEY")
Base64-encoded DER bytes (SPKI)
Hex-encoded DER bytes (SPKI)

remarks.para: Note: This method does not attempt to validate that the decoded bytes are a well-formed SPKI structure. If invalid bytes are provided, callers will typically fail verification deterministically (pin mismatch).

member.param: Public key input string.
member.returns: Decoded DER bytes for the public key (SPKI).

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.GetSpkiBytes(System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates.X509Certificate2)
member.summary: Returns the DER-encoded SubjectPublicKeyInfo (SPKI) bytes for a certificate public key.

remarks.para: SPKI (SubjectPublicKeyInfo) is the canonical public-key representation used by this library for pinning. It corresponds to PEM -----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----.
This method is used to enforce Law 6: public-key pinning hashes SPKI bytes, not X509Certificate2.GetPublicKey().

member.param: Certificate whose public key will be exported as SPKI.
member.returns: SPKI DER bytes, or an empty array if unavailable.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.FileSha256(System.String)
member.summary: Computes the SHA-256 hash of a file and returns the result as uppercase hexadecimal.
member.param: Path to the file to hash.
member.returns: Uppercase hexadecimal SHA-256 digest.
member.remarks: This method reads the entire file stream to compute the digest. Failures are reported as using file-system or cryptography error categories.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.Sha256Hex(System.IO.Stream,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Computes the SHA-256 hash of the provided stream and returns the result as uppercase hexadecimal.
member.param: The stream to hash.
If true and the stream supports seeking, the stream position is restored to its original value after hashing.
member.returns: Uppercase hexadecimal SHA-256 digest.
member.remarks: This method reads from the stream's current position to EOF. If is true, and the stream is seekable, the original position is restored after hashing.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.OpenReadLocked(System.String)
member.summary: Opens a file for read-only hashing/verification with restrictive sharing to reduce TOCTOU file-swap opportunities, and returns the open stream.
member.param: Path to the file.
member.returns: An open with and .
member.remarks: On Windows, prevents other processes from opening the file for write or delete while this stream is open (subject to privileges/OS policy). Failures are reported as .

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.Sha256Hex(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Computes the SHA-256 hash of the provided byte array and returns the result as uppercase hexadecimal.
member.param: The data to hash.
member.returns: Uppercase hexadecimal SHA-256 digest.
member.remarks: If is empty, the returned digest is the SHA-256 of an empty input.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.PublicKeySha256(System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates.X509Certificate2)
member.summary: Computes the SHA-256 hash of an X.509 certificate's SubjectPublicKeyInfo (SPKI) bytes and returns uppercase hex.
member.param: The certificate whose SPKI bytes will be hashed.
member.returns: Uppercase hexadecimal SHA-256 digest of the certificate SPKI bytes. Returns if is null or SPKI export is unavailable.

remarks.para: This helper defines the library's public-key pinning identity: SHA-256 over DER SubjectPublicKeyInfo (SPKI) bytes (PEM "BEGIN PUBLIC KEY").
Do not replace this with X509Certificate2.GetPublicKey(); that returns key bits, not SPKI, and will not match the --pin/--pubpin contract.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.FixedTimeEquals(System.Byte[],System.Byte[])
member.summary: Compares two byte arrays using a fixed-time comparison to reduce timing side-channel leakage.
member.param: First byte array.
Second byte array.
member.returns: true if the arrays are non-null, have the same length, and contain identical bytes; otherwise false.
member.remarks: Uses after validating lengths. Length mismatches return false without attempting comparison.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.HexBytesEquals(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Decodes two hexadecimal strings to bytes and compares them using fixed-time comparison.
member.param: First hexadecimal string.
Second hexadecimal string.
member.returns: true if the decoded byte sequences are identical; otherwise false.
member.remarks: Input normalization removes non-hex characters and uppercases letters before decoding. Invalid hex input is reported by the underlying decode helpers as .

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.GetHexValue(System.Char)
member.summary: Converts a single hexadecimal character into its numeric value.
member.param: A hexadecimal character in the range 0-9 or A-F. Lowercase characters must be normalized before calling this method.
member.returns: The integer value represented by the hexadecimal character (0–15).

remarks.para: This method performs strict validation and throws if the character is not a valid uppercase hexadecimal digit.
For flexible parsing scenarios, prefer or , which handle normalization and validation.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.TryDecodeHex(System.String,System.Byte[]@)
member.summary: Attempts to decode a hexadecimal string to bytes.
member.param: The hexadecimal string to decode.
On success, receives the decoded bytes; otherwise an empty array.
member.returns: true if decoding succeeded; otherwise false.
member.remarks: This method normalizes the input via before decoding. Any decode error is caught and reported as false.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.DecodeHex(System.String)
member.summary: Decodes a hexadecimal string to bytes after normalizing it.
member.param: The hexadecimal string to decode.
member.returns: The decoded bytes.
member.remarks: Normalization strips all non-hex characters and uppercases letters. An empty input results in an empty byte array. Invalid hex input is reported as .

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.EncodeHex(System.String)
member.summary: Encodes a UTF-8 string into uppercase hexadecimal.
member.param: The input string to encode.
member.returns: Uppercase hexadecimal representation of the UTF-8 bytes. Returns if is null.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.EncodeHex(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Encodes a byte array into uppercase hexadecimal.
member.param: The bytes to encode.
member.returns: Uppercase hexadecimal representation. Returns if is null or empty.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.NormalizeHex(System.String)
member.summary: Normalizes a string containing hexadecimal characters by stripping all non-hex characters and uppercasing letters.
member.param: The input string that may contain hex characters (and optionally other separators).
member.returns: A string consisting only of uppercase hex digits (0-9, A-F). Returns if is null/whitespace.
member.remarks: This is useful for accepting thumbprints or hashes that may include spaces, colons, or other formatting characters.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.ShortHash(System.String)
member.summary: Computes a short, deterministic identifier by taking SHA-256 of the UTF-8 input and returning the first 12 bytes as uppercase hex.
member.param: Input string to hash.
member.returns: 24-character uppercase hex string (12 bytes).
member.remarks: This is not intended for cryptographic authentication. It is intended for compact, readable identifiers where collision risk is low but non-zero.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.ProgramHandle
member.summary: Produces the platform-appropriate base program handle used to name service/task/app identifiers consistently across OS families.
member.returns: A handle string formatted for the current OS: Windows: service path-like (for example \MyWebExt\FileEngine), macOS: reverse-DNS (for example com.mywebext.fileengine), Linux/others: dot-separated (for example mywebext.fileengine).
member.remarks: The handle format is intentionally stable and OS-conventional to support predictable installation artifacts.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.ProgramHandle(System.String)
member.summary: Produces a platform-appropriate program handle by appending a sub-identifier to the base handle.
member.param: Sub-identifier to append (normalized to lowercase). Null is treated as empty.
member.returns: Windows: {base}\{p}; macOS/Linux: {base}.{p}.
member.remarks: This is typically used to create consistent identifiers for sub-components (for example "host", "client", "updater").

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.ProgramHandle(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Produces a platform-appropriate program handle from an organization and product name.
member.param: Organization identifier. If null/empty, defaults to mywebext.
Product identifier. If null/empty, defaults to app.
member.remarks: This helper supports generating stable identifiers for third-party developers while keeping OS conventions.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.HexEquals(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Compares two hex strings for equality after trimming, using case-insensitive comparison.
member.param: First hex string.
Second hex string.
member.returns: true if equal ignoring case and surrounding whitespace; otherwise false.
member.remarks: This is a convenience comparer; it is not fixed-time and should not be used where timing resistance is required.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.StringEsc(System.String)
member.summary: Removes newline characters from the provided string.
member.param: Input string.
member.returns: The string with \n and \r removed. Returns empty string if is null.
member.remarks: Intended for making values safe for single-line logging or deterministic serialization surfaces.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.XMLEncode(System.String)
member.summary: Encodes a string for XML contexts by escaping special characters.
member.param: Input string.
member.returns: An XML-escaped string. Returns empty string if is null.
member.remarks: This performs minimal entity encoding for XML attributes/text: &, <, >, ", '. It also attempts to avoid double-encoding existing entities by only encoding '&' when it is not already part of &name;.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.XMLDecode(System.String)
member.summary: Decodes common XML entities back into their character equivalents.
member.param: XML-escaped string.
member.returns: A decoded string. Returns empty string if is null.
member.remarks: This performs a simple replacement pass and does not validate XML or handle numeric entities beyond those explicitly implemented.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.HTMLEncode(System.String)
member.summary: Encodes a string for HTML contexts by escaping special characters.
member.param: Input string.
member.returns: An HTML-escaped string. Returns empty string if is null.
member.remarks: Escapes: &, <, >, ", and apostrophe as '. This is intended for basic output escaping and does not attempt full HTML sanitization.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.HTMLDecode(System.String)
member.summary: Decodes common HTML entities back into their character equivalents.
member.param: HTML-escaped string.
member.returns: A decoded string. Returns empty string if is null.
member.remarks: This performs a simple replacement pass and does not validate HTML or handle arbitrary/numeric entities beyond those explicitly implemented.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.Null(System.String)
member.summary: Determines whether a string is null, empty, or consists only of whitespace.
member.param: The string to test.
member.returns: true if null/empty/whitespace; otherwise false.
member.remarks: The annotation informs the compiler that when this method returns false, is not null.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.GetStringOrNull(System.Text.Json.JsonElement,System.String)
member.summary: Reads a JSON string property by name and returns its value, or null if the property is missing or not a JSON string.
member.param: The JSON element containing the property.
The property name.
member.returns: The string value if present and a JSON string; otherwise null.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.TryGetString(System.Text.Json.JsonElement,System.String,System.String@)
member.summary: Attempts to read a JSON string property by name.
member.param: The JSON element containing the property.
The property name.
On success, receives the string value; otherwise null.
member.returns: true if the property exists and is a JSON string with a non-null value; otherwise false.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.ToUtf8Bytes(System.String)
member.summary: Encodes a string as UTF-8 bytes.
member.param: Input string.
member.returns: UTF-8 bytes, or an empty array if is null.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.FromUtf8Bytes(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Decodes UTF-8 bytes into a string.
member.param: UTF-8 byte array.
member.returns: The decoded string, or if is null or empty.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.ReadAllBytesSafe(System.String)
member.summary: Reads all bytes from a file.
member.param: Path to the file.
member.returns: The file contents.
member.remarks: This is a thin wrapper over to keep call sites uniform. Failures are reported as .

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Functions.WriteAllBytesAtomic(System.String,System.Byte[])
member.summary: Writes bytes to a file using an atomic replace strategy to avoid partially-written outputs.
member.param: Destination file path.
Data to write.
member.remarks: Writes to {path}.tmp first, then replaces/moves into place. If the destination exists, is used; otherwise . The destination directory is created if needed. Failures are reported as .

member.name: P:CtxSignlib.Functions.OSName
member.summary: Gets a cached OS platform name for the current process.
member.value: One of: Windows, Linux, OSX, or FreeBSD.
member.remarks: The value is computed once and cached for subsequent calls.

member.name: P:CtxSignlib.Functions.IsWindows
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the current OS is Windows.

member.name: P:CtxSignlib.Functions.IsOSX
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the current OS is macOS.

member.name: P:CtxSignlib.Functions.IsLinux
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the current OS is Linux.

member.name: P:CtxSignlib.Functions.IsFreeBSD
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the current OS is FreeBSD.

member.name: T:CtxSignlib.Manifest.DetailedManifestFileVerification
member.summary: Performs strict manifest verification and returns a detailed categorized result.
member.remarks:
This class preserves strict verification semantics: listed files must be present, present files must hash-match, and unreadable existing files are fatal.
It does not replace or weaken . It adds a richer reporting surface for callers that need categorized file lists.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Manifest.DetailedManifestFileVerification.VerifyManifestDetailed(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Verifies all manifest-listed files in strict mode and returns only pass/fail.
member.param: The root directory that all manifest file paths must resolve under.
Full or relative path to the manifest JSON file. If relative, it is resolved under . Must resolve to a location inside .
member.returns: true if the result satisfies strict manifest verification semantics; otherwise false.
member.remarks: Strict mode means: missing file = fail, bad hash = fail, unreadable present file = fail.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Manifest.DetailedManifestFileVerification.VerifyManifestDetailedResult(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Verifies all manifest-listed files in strict mode and returns detailed categorized results.
member.param: The root directory that all manifest file paths must resolve under.
Full or relative path to the manifest JSON file. If relative, it is resolved under . Must resolve to a location inside .
member.returns: A detailed result containing passed, missing, failed, and unreadable file lists.
member.remarks:
This method applies strict verification semantics over the shared categorized manifest result model.
The returned value is computed using strict verification semantics.
Excluded files remain excluded exactly as they are in the existing manifest verification model.

member.name: T:CtxSignlib.Manifest.ManifestExclude
member.summary: Represents a manifest exclusion rule for a directory or file path, with an optional content-filtering regular expression.

remarks.para: Rules are interpreted by as follows:

item.description: If ends with /, it is treated as a directory prefix exclusion (all files under that prefix are excluded). Directory excludes must not define .
Otherwise it is treated as an exact file rule keyed by normalized manifest path.
If is null/empty for a file rule, the file is fully excluded.
If is present for a file rule, the file remains included but its hash is computed after applying the regex removal to UTF-8 decoded text content (non-UTF-8 content causes an error).

remarks.para: Paths are expected to be manifest-relative and are normalized to forward slashes by .

member.name: P:CtxSignlib.Manifest.ManifestExclude.Path
member.summary: Gets or sets the manifest-relative path for the exclusion rule.
member.remarks: Directory exclusions are indicated by a trailing / (for example bin/). File exclusions should be an exact manifest path (for example appsettings.json).

member.name: P:CtxSignlib.Manifest.ManifestExclude.Regex
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional regular expression pattern used for content filtering prior to hashing.
member.remarks: When set for a file rule, the file is still included in the manifest, but its SHA-256 hash is computed from the UTF-8 decoded text with all matches removed. Directory rules must not specify a regex.

member.name: T:CtxSignlib.Manifest.ManifestBuilder
member.summary: Provides methods for building or updating a manifest that records file hashes and supports exclusion rules.

remarks.para: The manifest output includes:

item.description: A deterministic list of files with SHA-256 hashes and sizes (ordering is deterministic; contents may change if files change).
An excludes array that may contain directory prefix exclusions and file exclusions (optionally with regex filtering).

remarks.para: Exclusion rules may be reused from an existing manifest (if present), and can be merged with an external, newline-delimited excludes file containing path-only entries.
Output ordering is deterministic: directory excludes first (sorted), then file excludes (sorted), then files sorted by path.
Note: the manifest includes createdUtc, which intentionally varies between runs.
Validation and operational failures are reported as .

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Manifest.ManifestBuilder.BuildOrUpdate(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Builds or updates a manifest at by hashing all files beneath , applying exclusion rules, and writing a JSON manifest.
member.param: Root directory to enumerate and hash.
Destination path for the manifest JSON. If relative, it is resolved under . If a manifest already exists here, its excludes array is reused (if present).
Optional path to a newline-delimited excludes file. Lines are treated as path-only exclude entries; blank lines and lines starting with # are ignored.
member.returns: The number of file entries written to the manifest.

remarks.para: Exclusion rules:

item.description: Directory excludes are specified by paths ending in /. They exclude all files under that prefix and must not specify regex.

File excludes are exact path rules. If the rule has no regex, the file is fully excluded. If the rule has a regex, the file remains included and its hash is computed after removing regex matches from UTF-8 text.

remarks.para: The manifest output file itself and its common signature companion name {manifest}.sig are excluded to prevent self-referential hashing.
Hash filtering requires the file to be valid UTF-8; otherwise an exception is thrown.

member.name: T:CtxSignlib.Manifest.ManifestPartialVerifier
member.summary: Verifies manifest-listed content in partial mode.
member.remarks: Partial mode preserves the current manifest parsing and path-trust rules, but changes file-presence semantics: missing files are reported and are non-fatal by themselves, while present files must still validate exactly. Files that cannot be safely verified due to invalid per-file syntax are also treated as verification failures.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Manifest.ManifestPartialVerifier.VerifyManifestPartial(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Verifies a manifest in partial mode.
member.param: Root directory that all manifest entries must resolve under.
Path to the manifest JSON file. If relative, it is resolved under . Must resolve to a location inside .
member.returns: true if the result satisfies partial manifest verification semantics; otherwise false.
member.remarks: Missing files are reported but do not fail partial verification by themselves. Present unreadable files, present hash-mismatched files, and files that cannot be safely verified due to invalid syntax all fail partial verification. Malformed manifests and trust-boundary violations still throw, matching the strict parser expectations.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Manifest.ManifestPartialVerifier.VerifyManifestPartialDetailed(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Verifies a manifest in partial mode and returns categorized file results.
member.param: Root directory that all manifest entries must resolve under.
Path to the manifest JSON file. If relative, it is resolved under . Must resolve to a location inside .
member.returns: A detailed partial verification result containing passed, missing, failed, unreadable, and invalid-syntax file lists.
member.remarks: Missing files are non-fatal in this mode. Present unreadable files, present hash-mismatched files, and files that cannot be safely verified due to invalid syntax are fatal. Malformed manifests and invalid trust-boundary inputs still throw. The returned value is computed using partial verification semantics.

member.name: T:CtxSignlib.Manifest.ManifestVerifier
member.summary: Performs strict manifest verification using the legacy API surface.
member.remarks:
This class preserves the original ctxsignlib strict verification API:
bool VerifyManifest(string rootDir, string manifestPath, out Dictionary<string,List<string>> failed)
Internally it now uses the shared ManifestVerificationCore engine and reconstructs the legacy grouped failure dictionary.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Manifest.ManifestVerifier.VerifyManifest(System.String,System.String,System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.String,System.Collections.Generic.List{System.String}}@)
member.summary: Verifies a manifest in strict mode and returns legacy grouped failure results.
member.param: Root directory that all manifest paths must resolve under.
Path to the manifest JSON file. If relative, it is resolved under .
Legacy failure dictionary grouped by expected SHA-256 value.
member.returns: True if strict manifest verification succeeds; otherwise false.

member.name: T:CtxSignlib.Manifest.SignedManifestPartialVerifier
member.summary: Verifies a signed manifest in partial mode.
member.remarks: This preserves the existing signed-manifest authentication contract: the manifest signature is verified first using public-key pinning, then authenticated manifest content is evaluated in partial mode.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Manifest.SignedManifestPartialVerifier.VerifySignedManifestPartial(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,CtxSignlib.Verify.VerifyResult@)
member.summary: Verifies a signed manifest in partial mode.
member.param: The root directory that the manifest and signature must reside under.
Path to the manifest JSON file. If relative, it is resolved under . Must resolve to a location inside .
Path to the detached signature file. If null or whitespace, defaults to {manifestPath}.sig. If relative, it is resolved under . Must resolve to a location inside .
Expected SHA-256 of the signer public key SPKI bytes, expressed as hex.
Receives the result of the detached CMS signature verification step.
member.returns: true if signature verification succeeds and the authenticated manifest result satisfies partial verification semantics; otherwise false.
member.remarks: Missing files are non-fatal in partial mode. Signature failure is always fatal. Files that cannot be safely verified due to invalid per-file syntax are also fatal to partial verification validity after authentication succeeds.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Manifest.SignedManifestPartialVerifier.VerifySignedManifestPartialDetailed(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,CtxSignlib.Verify.VerifyResult@)
member.summary: Verifies a signed manifest in partial mode and returns detailed categorized results.
member.param: The root directory that the manifest and signature must reside under.
Path to the manifest JSON file. If relative, it is resolved under . Must resolve to a location inside .
Path to the detached signature file. If null or whitespace, defaults to {manifestPath}.sig. If relative, it is resolved under . Must resolve to a location inside .
Expected SHA-256 of the signer public key SPKI bytes, expressed as hex. Non-hex characters are ignored during normalization.
Receives the result of the detached CMS signature verification step.
member.returns: A detailed partial verification result.
member.remarks: If signature verification fails, the method returns a failed result immediately and does not evaluate manifest file entries. Missing files are reported but are non-fatal in partial mode. Present unreadable files, present hash-mismatched files, and files that cannot be safely verified due to invalid syntax are fatal after authentication succeeds. The returned value is computed using partial verification semantics after signed-manifest authentication succeeds. Input and trust-boundary validation failures are reported as .

member.name: T:CtxSignlib.Manifest.SignedManifestVerifier
member.summary: Verifies a manifest using a detached CMS/PKCS#7 signature with public-key pinning, then verifies the manifest’s file hashes.

remarks.para: This class composes two verification steps:

item.description: Signature verification: validates a detached CMS signature over the manifest file and enforces a pinned public-key SHA-256 (--pubpin).
Content verification: validates all file hashes listed in the manifest (honoring manifest exclude rules).

remarks.para: Trust boundary:

item.description: manifestPath and sigPath must resolve to locations inside rootDir (otherwise this API throws).

Manifest file entries are treated as untrusted. The underlying manifest verifier enforces that each listed path resolves under rootDir; escaping paths are treated as failures (reported) rather than being accessed.

remarks.para: This API returns false if either step fails (signature invalid/missing/pin mismatch, or any manifest file verification failure).
Input and trust-boundary validation failures are reported as .

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Manifest.SignedManifestVerifier.VerifySignedManifest(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,CtxSignlib.Verify.VerifyResult@,System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.String,System.Collections.Generic.List{System.String}}@)
member.summary: Verifies a signed manifest by validating its detached CMS signature (with public-key pinning) and then validating the manifest’s file hashes.
member.param: The root directory that the manifest and signature must reside under, and that all manifest file entries must resolve under.
Path to the manifest JSON file. If relative, it is resolved under . Must resolve to a location inside .
Path to the detached signature file. If null/whitespace, defaults to {manifestPath}.sig. If relative, it is resolved under . Must resolve to a location inside .

The pinned signer identity in --pubpin form: SHA-256 of the signer's public key SPKI DER bytes (hex). This corresponds to the Laws in Functions.cs where --pubpin = SHA-256(--pin) and --pin is SPKI DER (PEM BEGIN PUBLIC KEY). Non-hex characters are ignored during normalization.
Receives the result code from signature verification (for example: Ok, SignatureMissing, BadSignature, WrongSigner).
Receives a grouped set of manifest file verification failures, keyed by expected SHA-256, with values listing the failing manifest-relative paths.
member.returns: true if the signature verifies successfully under the pinned public key and the manifest contents verify with zero file failures; otherwise false.

remarks.para: Return behavior:

item.description: If signature verification fails, the method returns false immediately and does not perform file verification.
If signature verification succeeds, the manifest file list is verified. Any missing/mismatching/escaping paths cause false, and details are returned in .

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Manifest.SignedManifestVerifier.VerifySignedManifest(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.String,System.Collections.Generic.List{System.String}}@)
member.summary: Verifies a signed manifest and returns only the file verification failures, omitting the signature result code.
member.param: The root directory that the manifest and signature must reside under, and that all manifest file entries must resolve under.
Path to the manifest JSON file. If relative, it is resolved under .
Path to the detached signature file. If null/whitespace, defaults to {manifestPath}.sig. If relative, it is resolved under .
--pubpin: SHA-256 of the signer's public key SPKI DER bytes (hex). Non-hex characters are ignored during normalization.
Receives a grouped set of manifest file verification failures, keyed by expected SHA-256, with values listing the failing manifest-relative paths.
member.returns: true if the signature verifies successfully under the pinned public key and the manifest contents verify with zero file failures; otherwise false.
member.remarks: This overload is a convenience wrapper that discards the signature status code. Use the full overload when you need to distinguish signature failures from file-hash failures.

member.name: T:CtxSignlib.Signing.CMSWriter
member.summary: Provides CMS / PKCS#7 detached signing helpers.

remarks.para: This class produces detached CMS (Cryptographic Message Syntax) signatures suitable for portable verification. The signer certificate is embedded (end-cert only) so verification can pin the signer using values extracted from the CMS itself (thumbprint / raw public key / public-key SHA-256) without consulting OS certificate stores.
This implementation does not add optional signed attributes such as signing time.

Note: CMS signature bytes are not guaranteed to be identical across runs for all key algorithms/providers (for example, ECDSA signatures are typically non-deterministic). The security contract of this library is based on deterministic verification and explicit signer pinning, not byte-for-byte signature reproducibility.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Signing.CMSWriter.SignDetachment(System.Byte[],System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates.X509Certificate2)
member.summary: Creates a detached CMS / PKCS#7 signature over the provided content bytes.
member.param: The content to sign.
The X.509 certificate containing an accessible private key used to generate the signature.
member.returns: Encoded CMS / PKCS#7 signature bytes (detached).

remarks.para: The signer certificate is embedded using (no chain). Verification is expected to enforce trust through explicit pinning against the signer contained in the CMS.
This method does not timestamp the signature and does not add signing-time attributes.
Failures are reported as .

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Signing.CMSWriter.SignDetachment(System.String,System.String,System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates.X509Certificate2)
member.summary: Creates a detached CMS / PKCS#7 signature for a file and writes it to disk.
member.param: Path to the file whose contents will be signed.
Destination path for the generated signature file.
The X.509 certificate containing an accessible private key used to generate the signature.
member.remarks: The content file is fully read into memory before signing. The signature file is written using to prevent partially-written outputs. Failures are reported as .

member.name: T:CtxSignlib.Verify.VerifyResult
member.summary: Represents the result of a CMS / PKCS#7 detached signature verification operation.
member.remarks: These values distinguish between missing inputs, cryptographic failures, and signer identity mismatches (pinning failures).

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Verify.VerifyResult.Ok
member.summary: Verification succeeded and the signer matched the pinned identity.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Verify.VerifyResult.ContentMissing
member.summary: The content to be verified was missing or not found.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Verify.VerifyResult.SignatureMissing
member.summary: The detached signature file or bytes were missing.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Verify.VerifyResult.BadSignature
member.summary: The CMS structure was invalid or cryptographic verification failed.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Verify.VerifyResult.NoSigner
member.summary: No signer certificate was present in the CMS message.

member.name: F:CtxSignlib.Verify.VerifyResult.WrongSigner
member.summary: The signature was cryptographically valid, but the signer identity did not match the pinned value.

member.name: T:CtxSignlib.Verify.CMSVerifier
member.summary: Provides detached CMS / PKCS#7 verification helpers with explicit signer pinning modes.

para.b: Contract (mirrors the immutable CLI laws in Functions.cs):

item.description: --thumb pins by signer certificate thumbprint (compares against the signer certificate embedded in the CMS signature).
--pin is the signer's raw public key bytes: DER SubjectPublicKeyInfo (SPKI), same bytes as PEM -----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----.
--pubpin is the SHA-256 of --pin (64 hex).

remarks.para: Verification is crypto-only: is called with verifySignatureOnly: true. No OS trust store validation is performed, and all pin comparisons are made against the signer embedded in the CMS signature.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Verify.CMSVerifier.VerifyDetachmentByThumbprint(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Verifies a detached CMS signature using a pinned signer certificate thumbprint.
member.param: Path to the original content file.
Path to the detached CMS signature file.
Expected signer certificate thumbprint (hex). Non-hex characters are ignored during normalization.
member.returns: A describing the outcome.
member.remarks: Thumbprint pinning ties verification to a specific certificate instance. For identity stability across certificate renewals that reuse the same key pair, prefer .

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Verify.CMSVerifier.VerifyDetachmentByThumbprint(System.Byte[],System.Byte[],System.String)
member.summary: Verifies a detached CMS signature using a pinned signer certificate thumbprint.
member.param: Original content bytes.
Detached CMS signature bytes.
Expected signer certificate thumbprint (hex).
member.returns: A describing the outcome.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Verify.CMSVerifier.VerifyDetachmentByPublicKey(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Verifies a detached CMS signature using a pinned SHA-256 of the signer public key (SPKI).
member.param: Path to the original content file.
Path to the detached CMS signature file.
Expected SHA-256 of the signer public key SPKI bytes (hex). Non-hex characters are ignored during normalization. This value corresponds to --pubpin.
member.returns: A describing the outcome.
member.remarks: This is the preferred pinning mode for long-lived trust because it remains valid across certificate renewals that reuse the same key pair.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Verify.CMSVerifier.VerifyDetachmentByPublicKey(System.Byte[],System.Byte[],System.String)
member.summary: Verifies a detached CMS signature using a pinned SHA-256 of the signer public key (SPKI).
member.param: Original content bytes.
Detached CMS signature bytes.
Expected SHA-256 of the signer public key SPKI bytes (hex). This value corresponds to --pubpin.
member.returns: A describing the outcome.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Verify.CMSVerifier.VerifyDetachmentByRawPublicKey(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Verifies a detached CMS/PKCS#7 signature and pins the signer by a raw public key (SPKI).

remarks.para: This method performs two checks:

item.description: Cryptographically verifies the detached CMS signature over .
Pins the signer identity by hashing the provided (SPKI / PEM "BEGIN PUBLIC KEY") with SHA-256 (producing the --pubpin value) and comparing it to the signer embedded in the CMS signature.

remarks.para: is NOT a secret. It is public key material and may be supplied as PEM, base64, or hex.

member.param: Path to the content file whose detached signature will be verified.
Path to the detached CMS signature file (e.g., *.sig) for .
The signer's raw public key bytes (DER SubjectPublicKeyInfo / SPKI), supplied as PEM, base64, or hex. This value corresponds to --pin.
member.returns: A indicating success () or the reason verification failed.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Verify.CMSVerifier.VerifyDetachmentOrThrow(System.Byte[],System.Byte[])
member.summary: Performs cryptographic verification of a detached CMS signature without any signer pinning.
member.param: Original content bytes.
Detached CMS signature bytes.
member.remarks: This method validates only the cryptographic integrity of the signature. It does not perform certificate chain validation or identity pinning. Failures are reported as .

member.name: T:CtxSignlib.Verify.ManifestEntryHashResolver
member.summary: Resolves how a manifest entry should be verified for a single target file and computes both the expected and actual SHA-256 values using the same exclude semantics as ManifestBuilder.

member.name: T:CtxSignlib.Verify.ManifestFileVerification
member.summary: Verifies a single file against an authenticated (signed) manifest in a TOCTOU-hardened way.

remarks.para: Workflow:

item.description: Verify the manifest's detached CMS signature (crypto-only, pinned signer).
Locate the file entry inside the manifest.
Compute the file hash using the same exclude semantics used by manifest creation.

remarks.para: This is not detached signature verification of the file bytes. It is file integrity validation using a signed manifest as the trust anchor.
Failure details are returned via the signatureResult out parameter and the failure out string:

item.description: signatureResult reports signature/identity failures (e.g., BadSignature, WrongSigner, ContentMissing).
failure reports manifest/path/hash failures (e.g., FileNotInManifest, HashMismatch).

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Verify.ManifestFileVerification.VerifyFileAgainstSignedManifest(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,CtxSignlib.Verify.VerifyResult@,System.String@,System.String@,System.String@)
member.summary: Verifies against a signed manifest using a pinned signer certificate thumbprint (--thumb).
member.param: Root directory that all verified files must reside under.
Path to the target file (absolute, or relative to ).
Path to the manifest JSON (absolute, or relative to ).
Optional path to the manifest detached signature file. If null/empty, defaults to {manifestPath}.sig.
Expected signer certificate thumbprint (hex). Non-hex characters are ignored during normalization.
Receives the result of validating the manifest signature and signer identity pin.
Receives the expected SHA-256 (hex) from the manifest for the target file.
Receives the actual SHA-256 (hex) computed from the target file.

Receives a deterministic failure code when signature validation succeeds but file/manifest checks fail: FileOutsideRoot, FileMissing, InvalidManifest, FileNotInManifest, HashMismatch. Empty string means no non-signature failure occurred.
member.returns: true if the manifest signature is valid (and pinned signer matches) and the file hash matches the manifest entry; otherwise false.
member.remarks: Signature verification is crypto-only and pins the signer extracted from the CMS signature itself (never OS stores). File hashing is delegated to , which applies the same manifest exclude semantics used during manifest creation, including regex-filtered hashing for matching file-level exclude rules. Input validation failures are reported as .

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Verify.ManifestFileVerification.VerifyFileAgainstSignedManifestByPublicKey(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,CtxSignlib.Verify.VerifyResult@,System.String@,System.String@,System.String@)
member.summary: Verifies against a signed manifest using a pinned public-key SHA-256 (--pubpin).
member.param: Root directory that all verified files must reside under.
Path to the target file (absolute, or relative to ).
Path to the manifest JSON (absolute, or relative to ).
Optional path to the manifest detached signature file. If null/empty, defaults to {manifestPath}.sig.
Expected SHA-256 of the signer's public key SPKI bytes (hex). This corresponds to --pubpin, where --pubpin = SHA-256(--pin) and --pin is SPKI DER (PEM BEGIN PUBLIC KEY).
Receives the result of validating the manifest signature and signer pin.
Receives the expected SHA-256 (hex) from the manifest for the target file.
Receives the actual SHA-256 (hex) computed from the target file.
Receives a deterministic failure code when signature validation succeeds but file/manifest checks fail: FileOutsideRoot, FileMissing, InvalidManifest, FileNotInManifest, HashMismatch.
member.returns: true if the manifest signature is valid (and pinned signer matches) and the file hash matches the manifest entry; otherwise false.
member.remarks: Signature verification is crypto-only and pins the signer extracted from the CMS signature itself (never OS stores). File hashing is delegated to , which applies the same manifest exclude semantics used during manifest creation, including regex-filtered hashing for matching file-level exclude rules. Input validation failures are reported as .

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Verify.ManifestFileVerification.VerifyFileAgainstSignedManifest(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,CtxSignlib.Verify.VerifyResult@,System.String@,System.String@,System.String@)
member.summary: Convenience overload: defaults the signature path to {manifestPath}.sig.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Verify.ManifestFileVerification.VerifyFileAgainstSignedManifestByPublicKey(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,CtxSignlib.Verify.VerifyResult@,System.String@,System.String@,System.String@)
member.summary: Convenience overload: defaults the signature path to {manifestPath}.sig.

member.name: T:CtxSignlib.Verify.PackageId
member.summary: Generates deterministic package identifiers from expected file identity data.

remarks.para: A package ID represents the identity of the full expected package content, not the current state of an installation.
Canonical entry format:
Entries are normalized, de-duplicated, sorted using , joined with LF, encoded as UTF-8, and hashed with SHA-256.
Two packages with the same included file paths and expected hashes will always produce the same package ID across platforms.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Verify.PackageId.Generate(CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ManifestPartialVerificationResult)
member.summary: Generates a deterministic package ID from the full expected package contents represented by a manifest verification result.
member.param: A manifest verification result whose expected-hash metadata represents the effective included package contents.
member.returns: Uppercase hexadecimal SHA-256 of the canonical package entry list. Returns the SHA-256 of an empty UTF-8 payload if no included entries are present.
member.remarks: Input validation failures are reported as .

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Verify.PackageId.Generate(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Generates a deterministic package ID from a single relative file path and expected SHA-256.
member.param: Relative file path.
Expected SHA-256 (hex) for the file.
member.returns: Uppercase hexadecimal SHA-256 of the canonical single-entry payload.
member.remarks: Input validation failures are reported as .

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Verify.PackageId.Generate(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.ValueTuple{System.String,System.String}})
member.summary: Generates a deterministic package ID from a sequence of relative file paths and expected SHA-256 values.
member.param: Sequence of tuples in the form (path, expectedSha256).
member.returns: Uppercase hexadecimal SHA-256 of the canonical entry payload.
member.remarks: Input validation failures are reported as .

member.name: T:CtxSignlib.Verify.RepairPackageId
member.summary: Generates deterministic repair package identifiers from manifest-governed file identity data.

remarks.para: A repair package ID is derived only from the manifest-relative file path and the expected SHA-256 hash of each file that must be satisfied by a package.

Failure reason is intentionally ignored. A missing file, unreadable file, or corrupt file produces the same repair package ID as long as the required file path and expected manifest hash are the same.
Canonical entry format:
Entries are normalized, de-duplicated, sorted using , joined with LF, encoded as UTF-8, and hashed with SHA-256.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Verify.RepairPackageId.Generate(CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ManifestPartialVerificationResult)
member.summary: Generates a deterministic repair package ID from the repair-relevant portions of a manifest verification result.
member.param: A manifest verification result containing categorized file state and expected-hash metadata.
member.returns: Uppercase hexadecimal SHA-256 of the canonical repair entry list. Returns the SHA-256 of an empty UTF-8 payload if no repair-relevant entries are present.
member.remarks:
This method includes:
are intentionally excluded.
Input and metadata validation failures are reported as .

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Verify.RepairPackageId.GenerateMissing(CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ManifestPartialVerificationResult)
member.summary: Generates a deterministic repair package ID from only the missing-file portion of a manifest verification result.
member.param: A manifest verification result containing expected-hash metadata.
member.returns: Uppercase hexadecimal SHA-256 of the canonical missing-file entry list.
member.remarks: Input and metadata validation failures are reported as .

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Verify.RepairPackageId.GenerateFailed(CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ManifestPartialVerificationResult)
member.summary: Generates a deterministic repair package ID from only the failed-file portion of a manifest verification result.
member.param: A manifest verification result containing expected-hash metadata.
member.returns: Uppercase hexadecimal SHA-256 of the canonical failed-file entry list.
member.remarks: Input and metadata validation failures are reported as .

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Verify.RepairPackageId.GenerateUnreadable(CtxSignlib.Diagnostics.ManifestPartialVerificationResult)
member.summary: Generates a deterministic repair package ID from only the unreadable-file portion of a manifest verification result.
member.param: A manifest verification result containing expected-hash metadata.
member.returns: Uppercase hexadecimal SHA-256 of the canonical unreadable-file entry list.
member.remarks: Input and metadata validation failures are reported as .

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Verify.RepairPackageId.Generate(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Generates a deterministic repair package ID from a single relative file path and expected SHA-256.
member.param: Relative file path.
Expected SHA-256 (hex) for the file.
member.returns: Uppercase hexadecimal SHA-256 of the canonical single-entry payload.
member.remarks: Input validation failures are reported as .

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Verify.RepairPackageId.Generate(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.ValueTuple{System.String,System.String}})
member.summary: Generates a deterministic repair package ID from a sequence of relative file paths and expected SHA-256 values.
member.param: Sequence of tuples in the form (path, expectedSha256).
member.returns: Uppercase hexadecimal SHA-256 of the canonical entry payload.
member.remarks: Input validation failures are reported as .

member.name: T:CtxSignlib.Verify.SingleFileVerification
member.summary: Provides convenience helpers for verifying a single signed file using detached CMS signatures.

remarks.para: These helpers wrap and provide simple pass/fail semantics while still returning the underlying for detailed inspection.
If sigPath is null or empty, it defaults to {contentPath}.sig.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Verify.SingleFileVerification.VerifyFile(System.String,System.String,System.String,CtxSignlib.Verify.VerifyResult@)
member.summary: Verifies a single signed file using thumbprint pinning (--thumb).
member.param: Path to the content file.
Path to the detached signature file. If null/empty, defaults to {contentPath}.sig.
Expected signer certificate thumbprint (hex). Non-hex characters are ignored during normalization.
Receives the detailed describing the verification outcome.
member.returns: true if verification succeeds and the signer matches the pinned thumbprint; otherwise false.
member.remarks: This method performs crypto-only verification and enforces explicit thumbprint pinning.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Verify.SingleFileVerification.VerifyFileByPublicKey(System.String,System.String,System.String,CtxSignlib.Verify.VerifyResult@)
member.summary: Verifies a single signed file using public-key SHA-256 pinning (--pubpin).
member.param: Path to the content file.
Path to the detached signature file. If null/empty, defaults to {contentPath}.sig.
Expected SHA-256 of the signer's public key SPKI bytes (hex). This value corresponds to --pubpin (where --pubpin = SHA-256(--pin) and --pin is SPKI DER).
Receives the detailed describing the verification outcome.
member.returns: true if verification succeeds and the signer matches the pinned public-key identity; otherwise false.
member.remarks: Public-key pinning is generally more stable than thumbprint pinning, as it remains valid across certificate renewals that reuse the same key pair.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Verify.SingleFileVerification.VerifyFile(System.String,System.String,CtxSignlib.Verify.VerifyResult@)
member.summary: Verifies a single signed file using thumbprint pinning and the default signature path.
member.param: Path to the content file.
Expected signer certificate thumbprint (hex).
Receives the detailed describing the verification outcome.
member.returns: true if verification succeeds and the signer matches the pinned thumbprint; otherwise false.
member.remarks: The signature file is assumed to be located at {contentPath}.sig.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Verify.SingleFileVerification.VerifyFileByPublicKey(System.String,System.String,CtxSignlib.Verify.VerifyResult@)
member.summary: Verifies a single signed file using public-key SHA-256 pinning and the default signature path.
member.param: Path to the content file.
Expected SHA-256 of the signer's public key SPKI bytes (hex). This corresponds to --pubpin.
Receives the detailed describing the verification outcome.
member.returns: true if verification succeeds and the signer matches the pinned public-key identity; otherwise false.
member.remarks: The signature file is assumed to be located at {contentPath}.sig.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Verify.SingleFileVerification.VerifyFileByRawPublicKey(System.String,System.String,System.String,CtxSignlib.Verify.VerifyResult@)
member.summary: Verifies a single signed file using raw public-key pinning (--pin).
member.param: Path to the content file.
Path to the detached signature file. If null/empty, defaults to {contentPath}.sig.
The signer's raw public key in SPKI form (DER SubjectPublicKeyInfo), supplied as PEM (BEGIN PUBLIC KEY), base64, or hex. This corresponds to --pin and is NOT a secret.
Receives the detailed describing the verification outcome.
member.returns: true if verification succeeds and the signer matches the pinned raw public key; otherwise false.
member.remarks: This method derives --pubpin deterministically as SHA-256(SPKI) and verifies via public-key pinning.

member.name: M:CtxSignlib.Verify.SingleFileVerification.VerifyFileByRawPublicKey(System.String,System.String,CtxSignlib.Verify.VerifyResult@)
member.summary: Verifies a single signed file using raw public-key pinning and the default signature path.
member.param: Path to the content file.
The signer's raw public key SPKI bytes, supplied as PEM/base64/hex.
Receives the detailed describing the verification outcome.
member.returns: true if verification succeeds; otherwise false.
member.remarks: The signature file is assumed to be located at {contentPath}.sig.
